title: The One With Phoebes Cookies

jokes.text: Chandler gets glasses and his friends don't notice
jokes.setup: Chandler excitedly asks what they think about his new look
jokes.punchline: His friends are completely oblivious to his new glasses
jokes.explanation: Classic Friends character moment - Chandler's insecurity meets his friends' obliviousness. Well-structured with good economy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So, what do you guys think? New look!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You look great!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, looking good!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Love it!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: My glasses! I got new glasses!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, nice!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Really suits you.

jokes.text: Eyes. No, no. Your eyes! No. Chandler's eyes!
jokes.setup: Friend trying to give hints about what changed
jokes.punchline: Increasingly frantic and specific attempts to direct attention to Chandler's eyes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure but fairly predictable sitcom beat

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Hey, look at Chandler!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Just... look at him. Really look.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, dude, there's something different.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What are you guys talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Your face! Look at your face!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What about my face?!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Your eyes! Look at your EYES!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: They're right there! In the middle of your face! The round things!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Those two organs that help you see! Below your eyebrows! Above your cheekbones!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I KNOW WHERE MY EYES ARE!

jokes.text: You always had glasses. No, I didn't. Are you sure?
jokes.setup: Friend insists Chandler always had glasses
jokes.punchline: Chandler's incredulous denial creates awkward moment
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom misunderstanding, functional but unremarkable

dialogue.speaker: Friend
dialogue.line: You've always worn glasses, Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? No, I haven't! I've never worn glasses in my life!

dialogue.speaker: Friend
dialogue.line: Yes, you have. I remember it clearly.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's insane. Why would you say that?

jokes.text: They were really round and burgundy, and they made you look kind of.... Feminine. Yes! No.
jokes.setup: Description of glasses building to compliment
jokes.punchline: Monica enthusiastically agrees with 'feminine,' Chandler denies
jokes.explanation: Well-structured setup with good character-specific reactions

dialogue.speaker: Friend
dialogue.line: Those are nice glasses. Very feminine.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Thank you! I think so too.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: They are not feminine.

jokes.text: If someone wants to give us a present, we can't deprive them of that joy.
jokes.setup: Monica says they don't need gifts
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mock-noble justification for wanting presents
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line - selfish but delivered with faux-altruism. Classic character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no, no. We do need gifts. How else will people know we exist?

jokes.text: Unless someone borrowed it and left it at the gynecologist. Yeah, and by 'someone' she means Joey.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions missing CD player at gynecologist
jokes.punchline: Chandler identifies Joey as the culprit
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - perfectly captures Joey's oblivious borrowing habits and Monica's passive-aggressive reveal

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I can't believe my CD player is missing from my gynecologist's office.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I hate to break it to you, but I'm pretty sure Joey stole it.

jokes.text: What we want, honey. No, you don't want this.
jokes.setup: Monica corrects to 'what we want'
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately contradicts the unity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing relationship dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What we want.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What I want.

jokes.text: Dying people say the craziest things.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions deathbed promise to grandmother
jokes.punchline: Phoebe casually dismisses deathbed promises
jokes.explanation: Peak Phoebe - darkly funny, perfectly delivered understatement that's both shocking and logical for her character

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I made a deathbed promise to my grandmother.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, those don't count.

jokes.text: Our kids are gonna be fat, aren't they?
jokes.setup: Monica talks about making world's best cookies
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact prediction about their future children
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with Chandler's typical dry delivery

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm making the world's best cookies.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, and our kids are gonna be really messed up.

jokes.text: Why would I do that? It took three guys to get the thing in there.
jokes.setup: Asked why he doesn't take boat out of marina
jokes.punchline: Joey's logic about effort required to move it
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey logic - misunderstanding the basic concept of owning a boat

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, it's in the marina. You know how hard it is to get it out of there? You gotta untie it, push it, get it in the water... I'm not doing all that work just to go out on a boat for like an hour.

jokes.text: It's good that you finally have a place to do that.
jokes.setup: Joey describes boat as place to sit, drink beer, eat chips
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically notes Joey finally has a place for these activities
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered sarcasm pointing out Joey does these things everywhere anyway

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Finally, a place where you can sit, drink beer, and eat chips.

jokes.text: Your own boat? He was trying to cheer me up. My pony was sick.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentions her dad bought her a boat at 15
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was to cheer her up because her pony was sick
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - starts with impressive boat ownership, reveals even more privileged backstory. Perfect Rachel character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: My dad bought me a boat when I was 15.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: A boat? That's amazing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, he did it to cheer me up because my pony was sick.

jokes.text: You know what I was thinking? What? Nothing, I just like to go like this.
jokes.setup: Monica asks what Chandler was thinking
jokes.punchline: He admits he just likes making the 'thinking' gesture
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about thinking gestures

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What were you thinking about?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, nothing. I just like doing this.

jokes.text: He doesn't ask just anyone to play. He didn't ask for you. He asked for 'Chauncey.' I assumed he meant you.
jokes.setup: Monica says her dad likes Chandler enough to invite him to play
jokes.punchline: Reveals dad asked for 'Chauncey,' not Chandler
jokes.explanation: Great setup and payoff - builds up Chandler's excitement then deflates it perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Dad really likes you. He asked if you wanted to play golf with him next weekend.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really? That's great!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, he specifically asked for Chauncey.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Chauncey? My name is Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: Or you could sit on the porch and make sure no one steals the trashcans. He does that every week too.
jokes.setup: Suggesting Chandler could play racquetball weekly
jokes.punchline: Alternative activity reveals more about Monica's dad's routine
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Monica's father's quirky habits

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler could play racquetball every week.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Or he could go to a hotel room every afternoon like my dad.

jokes.text: You're not a lefty? Does anybody know me?
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions playing with his left hand
jokes.punchline: Chandler's exasperated question after Ross doesn't know his handedness
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the glasses theme - Chandler's friends don't know basic things about him

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm left-handed!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, you're not.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes, I am!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, you're not. You're right-handed. You always have been.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How do you know what hand I write with?

jokes.text: No! Why didn't you make a copy and keep it in a fireproof box and keep it at least 100 yards away from the original?
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions fire burned the recipe
jokes.punchline: Monica's obsessively detailed backup plan suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - her obsessive planning applied to something that already happened. Great character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, don't panic. I have a backup plan. I have a backup plan for my backup plan. And a backup plan for that backup plan. I actually have a color-coded binder system with laminated copies of all my recipes stored in a safe deposit box at the bank, in a fireproof safe, with copies also kept at my parents' house, in my apartment, in my office at work, and memorized. All memorized.

jokes.text: Because I'm normal.
jokes.setup: Monica's elaborate backup plan suggestion
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's simple, deadpan response
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to Monica's neurosis. Great economy and timing.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Or we could just not do it.

jokes.text: An old cookie? This is what happens when you don't register for gifts.
jokes.setup: Phoebe offers old frozen cookie as gift
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic comment about gift registry
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about wedding gift expectations

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh wow, a frozen cookie. You know, I've been looking everywhere for this. I checked Pottery Barn, I checked Williams-Sonoma, I even checked the gift registry at Restoration Hardware. But nothing — nothing compared to a cookie that's been in your freezer since the Clinton administration.

jokes.text: We can't accept this. Why not? Because it's gross.
jokes.setup: Chandler says they can't accept the cookie
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt honesty about why
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Monica's directness cuts through Chandler's politeness

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We can't accept this.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because it's too generous.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, it's because you're fat and you can't have any more cookies.

jokes.text: Two! I've been engaged twice.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions owing Ross an engagement present
jokes.punchline: Ross points out he's been engaged twice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment referencing Ross's romantic history

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Twice! I've been engaged twice!

jokes.text: Who names their boat Coast Guard, anyway? That is the Coast Guard.
jokes.setup: Joey complains about boat named Coast Guard
jokes.punchline: Rachel reveals it actually is the Coast Guard
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his cluelessness about boats leads to great setup/payoff structure

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What is this thing? 'Coast Guard'? What kind of a name is that for a boat?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, that IS the Coast Guard.

jokes.text: The coast is all the way over there.
jokes.setup: Rachel explains it's the Coast Guard
jokes.punchline: Joey's confused geography
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to Coast Guard joke showing Joey's continued confusion

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, the Coast Guard? What coast?

jokes.text: Boat rope? Wrong! How do you get the mainsail up? Rub it?
jokes.setup: Rachel quizzes Joey on sailing terms
jokes.punchline: Joey's hilariously wrong answer about raising mainsail
jokes.explanation: Great Joey logic - applying completely wrong method to sailing. Good escalation of his cluelessness.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, what does 'raising the mainsail' mean?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Uh, when the captain yells, 'All hands on deck!' and you have to go get him a sandwich?

jokes.text: I say, 'Come again?' I know this one. I know this one.
jokes.setup: Asked what to do when Rachel says 'coming about'
jokes.punchline: Joey's inappropriate interpretation and false confidence
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey innuendo humor, fits character but fairly predictable

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Just smile and nod. Pretend you know what she's talking about.

jokes.text: I want to make a ship-to-shore call to Chandler.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks if he wants to pay attention or die
jokes.punchline: Joey's completely unrelated response about calling Chandler
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Joey completely ignores the serious question to focus on ship terminology

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Do you want to pay attention or die?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I should call Chandler.

jokes.text: That and arrogance.
jokes.setup: Monica explains difference between professional and layman
jokes.punchline: Chandler adds arrogance as the other difference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler timing and delivery - cuts Monica down with perfect economy

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: The difference between a professional and a layman is that a professional knows all the rules and regulations.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And arrogance. Don't forget arrogance.

jokes.text: Women are mean!
jokes.setup: Joey returns from sailing lesson
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration about women
jokes.explanation: Standard Joey reaction, fits character but not particularly creative

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I just realized something. Women are like boats.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You gotta know how to handle 'em, treat 'em right, and sometimes you gotta just hold on and hope you don't capsize.

jokes.text: I wish you a long and happy marriage.
jokes.setup: Looking at tiny crumb of grandmother's cookie
jokes.punchline: Phoebe addresses the crumb as if it's the whole gift
jokes.explanation: Sweet Phoebe moment - treating the inadequate gift with full ceremony

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh wow, thank you so much! This is exactly what I needed. You shouldn't have!

jokes.text: Chandler accidentally sits on Monica's dad in steamy sauna
jokes.setup: Chandler enters steamy room without glasses
jokes.punchline: Sits on what he thinks is bench but is actually Monica's father
jokes.explanation: Major physical comedy moment with great setup from glasses subplot. High cringe factor.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Ahhhh! Oh my God! Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: What the—Chandler?!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Jack! I'm so sorry, I couldn't see, I thought you were a bench!

jokes.text: You gave my father a lap dance.
jokes.setup: The sauna incident
jokes.punchline: Monica's horrified interpretation of what happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the physical comedy - Monica's dramatic interpretation makes it worse

jokes.text: Because otherwise they'd have to call it the 'room' room.
jokes.setup: Asked why there's so much steam in steam room
jokes.punchline: Chandler's logical but silly explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deflection with wordplay after embarrassing situation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I would think it's because they're steaming the room. It's kind of in the name. Steam room. If it was called the 'slightly warm room,' I'd be very upset right now.

jokes.text: I know you wanted to bond with my dad, but did you have to bond to that part?
jokes.setup: Discussion of the inappropriate touching
jokes.punchline: Monica's pun on 'bond' and physical contact
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that maintains the cringe while adding linguistic humor

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I guess you could say we're really bonding!

jokes.text: He still tells the story about how Monica tried to escape from fat camp. I wasn't escaping. Then how'd you get caught in the barbed wire? I was trying to help out a squirrel. You were trying to eat it.
jokes.setup: Discussion of embarrassing family stories
jokes.punchline: Escalating reveals about Monica's fat camp incident
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure - each reveal makes Monica's story worse. Perfect character history detail.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh come on, Monica's fat camp story has to be the most embarrassing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It was ONE summer!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: One summer? You were there for three months and came back with a color.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I lost fifteen pounds!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And found them all again by September.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? At least I went to fat camp. You went to band camp and came back able to play the recorder badly.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey, I was very popular at band camp.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, with the other people who couldn't get dates.

jokes.text: Aren't you a little overdressed? You better make sure he tips you this time.
jokes.setup: Chandler leaving dressed up to see Monica's dad
jokes.punchline: Ross and Monica's lap dance jokes
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the sauna incident with escalating embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey, where are you going dressed like that?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm going to see Monica's dad.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: In that outfit? He's going to think you're a stripper.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, don't say that! He'll think Chandler's going to give him a lap dance!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, is he?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No!

jokes.text: And by 'face,' I don't mean his lap. And by 'face,' I don't mean my ass.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying he'll talk to dad 'face to face'
jokes.punchline: Clarifying which faces he means given the lap dance incident
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke where Chandler addresses the obvious double meaning

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm going to talk to my dad face to face.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Which faces?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What do you mean 'which faces'?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, after the lap dance incident...

jokes.text: Hey, they didn't get us anything. Thank you!
jokes.setup: Discussion about getting Monica and Chandler engagement gifts
jokes.punchline: Phoebe and Rachel's mutual relief at not needing to buy gifts
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment about gift-giving obligations

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So we don't have to get them an engagement gift?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, we already got them the wedding gift.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh thank God. I spent all my money on a new bra.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Phoebe, that's... okay, never mind.

jokes.text: I don't know the boat way to say this, but never!
jokes.setup: Asked when they're getting back on the water
jokes.punchline: Joey's attempt to speak in nautical terms while declining
jokes.explanation: Good Joey attempt at nautical language while being dramatic

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I would love to, but I can't. I'm afraid of the water. Well, not the water itself, but what's in it. You know, like fish and... sea monsters and... water.

jokes.text: But you couldn't move because you were wearing three life jackets. You have to respect the sea.
jokes.setup: Joey claiming Rachel wouldn't let him help on her dad's boat
jokes.punchline: Rachel reveals Joey was wearing three life jackets, Joey's serious response
jokes.explanation: Great escalation revealing Joey's extreme overcaution, followed by his earnest justification

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, you were wearing three life jackets!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So?

jokes.text: Six and a half. You knocked the last one out of my hand, remember?
jokes.setup: Rachel says Joey drank seven beers
jokes.punchline: Joey's precise correction about the incomplete beer
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey priorities - correcting the beer count rather than addressing the problem

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, you drank seven beers!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Eight. There's a half-empty one in the kitchen.

jokes.text: You'll be topless? Joey! You want me to learn?
jokes.setup: Rachel agreeing to be nicer during sailing lessons
jokes.punchline: Joey pushes his luck with inappropriate request
jokes.explanation: Predictable Joey horniness but delivered with good timing

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I'll be nicer during sailing lessons.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Great! So does that mean you'll finally let me see your—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You didn't even let me finish!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I didn't have to.

jokes.text: What for, a bake sale? No, just a Friday night.
jokes.setup: Monica mentioning making lots of cookies in 9th grade
jokes.punchline: Monica's admission about her eating habits as a teenager
jokes.explanation: Good character moment referencing Monica's past weight struggles with matter-of-fact delivery

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I made a lot of cookies in ninth grade.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, I remember. I gained fifteen pounds just watching you eat them.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I didn't eat all of them!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, you ate most of them.

jokes.text: Batch 16. Sixteen, people, get out of the way!
jokes.setup: Discussion about which cookie batch was bad
jokes.punchline: Phoebe dramatically spitting out the cookie
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with dramatic announcement, good escalation of the cookie testing

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, this one tastes like a tire fire had a baby with a shoe!

jokes.text: Why not just say left? Okay, go to the left. The left. Just sit over there!
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to use nautical terms with Joey
jokes.punchline: Rachel's increasing frustration leading to giving up on proper sailing terms
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Rachel's teaching method breaking down

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, so you're telling me you don't know what 'starboard' means?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Is that like... a board made of stars?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No! It's the right side of the ship!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh! Why didn't you just say that?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Because it's a nautical term, Joey. It's called 'starboard.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, what about the left side?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That's 'port.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Like the wine?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No! It's just... it's port. The left side is port.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's confusing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yes, well, welcome to nautical terminology. Now, if we're sailing and I say 'hard to port,' you go left, okay?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Why can't you just say left?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Because— you know what? Fine. Left. It's left. We're done here.

jokes.text: We did that when that bird flew over with the fish in his mouth. Did you see it? It was gross.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to review sailing terms
jokes.punchline: Joey completely distracted by nature observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - completely missing the point to focus on random distraction

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, so a jib is the triangular sail in front, and the mainsail is the large one behind it.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Did you see that squirrel outside? That thing just ran up the tree like it owned the place.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, focus. We're talking about sailing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I am focused. Look at how fluffy its tail is! That's a $10,000 tail right there.

jokes.text: I did not see the bird or the fish! I did not see the piece of Styrofoam shaped like Mike Tyson!
jokes.setup: Joey describing distractions during sailing lesson
jokes.punchline: Rachel's increasingly frustrated list of things Joey saw instead of paying attention
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with absurd specific detail (Mike Tyson Styrofoam). Shows Rachel's complete exasperation.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, you were supposed to be learning to sail!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Instead you were staring at that girl in the bikini.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Then you got distracted by the hot dog stand.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Then you were watching that guy's yacht like you owned it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And don't even get me started on the time you spent looking at seagulls!

jokes.text: Greens don't quit! Greens? I'm a Tribbiani. Tribbianis quit.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to motivate Joey not to quit
jokes.punchline: Joey pointing out family differences in quitting philosophy
jokes.explanation: Great character moment contrasting family values. Joey's matter-of-fact acceptance of being a quitter is perfectly delivered.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, you can't quit! You're so close to making it!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, but my family doesn't quit things. We just... stop showing up.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I just said, 'Greens don't quit,' didn't I?
jokes.setup: Rachel realizing she's acting like her father
jokes.punchline: Her horror at becoming like her controlling parent
jokes.explanation: Good character development moment showing Rachel's self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God. I'm turning into my father.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I just told Monica she couldn't wear that outfit because it wasn't appropriate for brunch.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That is exactly what he would say.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm becoming a controlling nightmare.

jokes.text: No, I'm not yelling at you. I'm just yelling near you.
jokes.setup: Joey accusing Rachel of yelling
jokes.punchline: Rachel's distinction between yelling at vs near someone
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Rachel's denial about her behavior

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You're yelling at me!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm not yelling at you, I'm yelling near you. It's very different.

jokes.text: I know port is right. Left. Damn it!
jokes.setup: Joey demonstrating what he learned
jokes.punchline: Still getting basic sailing terms wrong
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Joey hasn't actually learned anything despite the reconciliation

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, so I learned all about sailing. You take the boat out on the water, you pull on the... the ropey things.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: The ropes?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, the ropes. And then you gotta watch out for the... the big swooshy thing that swings around and hits you.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: The boom?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Sure, the boom. And if you're lucky, you'll make it to the other side without the boat doing that tippy thing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Capsizing?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's probably what the professionals call it, yeah.

jokes.text: Looking up? She was really nice to me, but she's in hell for sure.
jokes.setup: Monica saying grandmother is looking down and smiling
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact correction about grandmother's afterlife location
jokes.explanation: Peak Phoebe - casually delivers dark humor about her grandmother's damnation while being affectionate. Perfect character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, she's not. She's in the radiator.

jokes.text: And that's not happening till October 15, 2032. That's the day you're gonna die? I've got shuffleboard that day.
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions talking to Ursula over her dead body
jokes.punchline: Phoebe has planned her death date around shuffleboard
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Phoebe logic - having a specific death date scheduled around recreational activity. Original and perfectly delivered.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm not talking to Ursula over my dead body.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I've planned my death date around shuffleboard.

jokes.text: That's what you think.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's shuffleboard plans for death day
jokes.punchline: Chandler's ominous response suggesting other plans
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to Phoebe's death plans with understated threat

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: When I die, I want to come back as a chair.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why a chair?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Because that way I can watch people sit on me all day.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's the saddest thing I've ever heard. Also, I have some bad news about your death day.

jokes.text: My grandmother said she got it from her grandmother, 'Nestley Toulouse.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining family recipe origins
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation of 'Nestlé Toll House' as French name
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the big reveal - Phoebe's innocent mispronunciation

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: My grandmother's secret recipe comes from a very famous French chef. Nestlé Toll House.

jokes.text: You Americans always butcher the French language.
jokes.setup: Monica correcting 'Nestlé Toll House'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe accusing Americans of butchering French when she's the one who was wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - completely missing the point while being indignant about it

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, Americans! You always butcher the French language. It's 'Nestlé Toll House,' not 'Nestle Toll House.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, that's what I just said.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, you said it wrong. The way you pronounced it was very American and very ugly.

jokes.text: You see, it is stuff like this which is why you're burning in hell!
jokes.setup: Realizing the recipe was store-bought all along
jokes.punchline: Phoebe addressing her dead grandmother about deception
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the hell comment with Phoebe's logic about why grandmother deserves damnation

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Grandma, I'm sorry. I have to tell you something. Your secret recipe? It was store-bought the whole time. I found the label.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I know, I know. You lied to us. But Grandma, you taught me that honesty is important, so I'm being honest with you now. You're a big fat liar!

jokes.text: So if you ever see me giving them legal advice, just nod along.
jokes.setup: Jack admitting he lied about being a lawyer to Monica's mother's family
jokes.punchline: Request for Chandler to go along with ongoing deception
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jack's confession showing the ongoing nature of his lie

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: Look, I may have told them I was a lawyer.

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: Okay, I definitely told them I was a lawyer.

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: Chandler, buddy, I'm gonna need you to back me up here.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What do you want me to say?

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: Just tell them you work with me. Tell them you're a lawyer too.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I can't do that, Jack.

jokes.text: Sandwiches! What else?
jokes.setup: Rope apparently connected to sandwich delivery system
jokes.punchline: Joey's enthusiasm for boat's real purpose
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Joey's use of boat as floating restaurant

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Could this BE any more perfect? A boat with a sandwich delivery system!

jokes.text: Not like that. You're letting all the good stuff fall out. Careful! You're wasting good pastrami!
jokes.setup: Someone eating sandwich incorrectly
jokes.punchline: Joey's increasing distress about wasted food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his food obsession overriding social norms

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! What are you doing? You're eating that sandwich all wrong!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You're supposed to eat it like this. See? You're wasting perfectly good food here.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Do you know how many people would kill for a sandwich like that? And you're just... mangling it!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: This is breaking my heart. This is actually breaking my heart right now.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I'm my dad!
jokes.setup: Joey being controlling about sandwich eating
jokes.punchline: Joey's realization about becoming like his father
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the theme of becoming your parents, Joey's version focused on food control

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Am I becoming my father?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh my God. I yelled at someone for eating my sandwich. That's exactly what my dad would do.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm turning into him. Next thing you know, I'll be leaving my family and taking up with a woman half my age.

tmdb_overview: Monica tries to figure out Phoebe's grandmother's secret chocolate chip cookie recipe. Meanwhile, Rachel tries to teach Joey how to sail, and Chandler has an awkward encounter when he tries to bond with Monica's father.
insight: Phoeebe's cookie obsession sustains 60 jokes at steady 3.4-per-minute clip, just missing elite tier.
